seo.title: Nebraska Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Nebraska — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license nebraska, dental assistant certification nebraska, RDA nebraska, DANB CDA nebraska, dental assistant radiography nebraska

board.name: Nebraska DHHS
board.mailing_address:
Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services
Licensure Unit
PO Box 94986
Lincoln, NE 68509-4986

license_types.title: Licensed Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Nebraska.

steps.title: Complete a Board-Approved Nitrous Oxide Monitoring Course
steps.description: Successfully complete a Board-approved course specific to the monitoring of nitrous oxide administration. Ensure the course meets the requirements outlined in Title 172 NAC 71 of the Nebraska Rules and Regulations for the Board of Dentistry.

steps.title: Download the Certification Application Form
steps.description: Obtain the official 'Dental Assistant - Nitrous Oxide Monitoring Certification Application' (Form 16-21) from the Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS) Dentistry Forms page.

steps.title: Complete Application and Gather Documentation
steps.description: Fill out the application form completely and accurately. Attach all required documentation, including official proof of completion from your Board-approved nitrous oxide monitoring course.

steps.title: Prepare Certification Fee
steps.description: Include the non-refundable initial certification fee of $25. This fee should be submitted with your completed application.

steps.title: Mail Application and Fee
steps.description: Mail the completed application form, all supporting documents, and the fee to the Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services, Licensure Unit, at PO Box 94986, Lincoln, NE 68509-4986.

steps.title: Receive Certification
steps.description: Allow time for processing by the Licensure Unit. Once approved, the Board will issue your Nitrous Oxide Monitoring Certification.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Licensed Dental Assistant through the Nebraska DHHS by license.

faqs.question: Does Nebraska require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Nebraska requires Licensed Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Nebraska?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Nebraska.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Nebraska?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Nebraska?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $95. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Licensed Dental Assistant through the Nebraska DHHS by license.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Nebraska
hero.dek: Nebraska does not require general licensing for Dental Assistants. However, dental assistants performing specific functions, such as monitoring nitrous oxide administration, must obtain certification by submitting a paper application (Form 16-21) to the Nebraska DHHS Licensure Unit.

official_links.portal_name: Nebraska DHHS Licensure Unit (Paper Application for Nitrous Oxide Monitoring Certification)
official_links.registration_steps: There is no online registration system for general Dental Assistant licensing in Nebraska. To apply for Nitrous Oxide Monitoring Certification, download the official application form (Form 16-21) from the Nebraska DHHS website, complete it, and mail it with the required fee to the Licensure Unit.
